channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Tea Party movement finds a new champion in Trump
item.description: Tea Party stalwarts who have seen their movement fizzle are finding a new champion in President Trump and see his second administration as a culmination and validation of their efforts. Sen. Ron Johnson (R-Wis.), who gave his first political speech at a Tea Party rally in Oshkosh, Wis.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Noem taps new ICE leaders and moves to identify leakers
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Sunday announced new leadership at the agency tasked with immigration enforcement as she also pledged to step up lie detector tests on employees to identify those who may be leaking information about operations to the media.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: D.C.'s Black Lives Matter mural will be erased. Look back at the iconic street painting
item.description: Over the past five years, Washington, D.C.'s iconic Black Lives Matter street painting has served as a powerful symbol of activism and a gathering place for joy and resistance.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Trump's speech to Congress ends notion that Dems are the 'compassionate' party after 92 years: expert
item.description: Former speechwriters for previous presidential administrations weighed in on President Donald Trump’s expansive speech before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its employees
item.description: Most of the 80,000 federal workers responsible for researching diseases, inspecting food and administering Medicare and Medicaid under the auspices of the Health and Human Services Department were emailed an offer to leave their job for as much as a $25,000 payment.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mayor Adams’ approval rating reaches record low ahead of election
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – The mayor’s race is heating up just as Mayor Eric Adams’ approval rating hits a new low, according to a new poll from Quinnipiac University. A new poll shows only 20% of voters approve of how Adams is handling his post, while 67% disapprove.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Musk and DOGE try to slash government by cutting out those who answer to voters
item.description: Conservatives have long talked about cutting government, but Republicans in Congress have shied away from approving deep reductions because of fear of how voters would react.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Travel headaches show need for feds to focus on airline tech jobs as true major delay culprit revealed
item.description: Airline safety is back in the news after several tragic incidents in US skies, and airline maintenance jobs are now at a premium, experts and lawmakers said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Dakota law raises questions about future of massive Midwest pipeline
item.description: A new law in South Dakota prohibiting the use of eminent domain to acquire land for carbon capture pipelines challenges the viability of a proposed project snaking through five Midwest states.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Raza And Jack Dura, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China learned from Trump's first trade war and changed its tactics when tariffs came again
item.description: The leaders of both Canada and Mexico got on the phone with President Donald Trump this past week to seek solutions after he slapped tariffs on their countries, but China’s president appears unlikely to make a similar call soon.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: JD Vance says he was confronted by pro-Ukrainian protesters while walking with toddler daughter
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Saturday he was accosted by pro-Ukrainian protesters who scared his 3-year-old daughter a week after his tense exchange with the Ukrainian president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House GOP seeks spending cuts, defense increases to avert shutdown
item.description: The bill's rollout kicks off a crucial stretch for GOP leadership to lock down support ahead of Friday's shutdown deadline with a Trump-backed strategy that has drawn the ire of Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Al Green censure highlights increased appetite for retribution in House
item.description: A formal House reprimand didn't contain the fallout from Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) interrupting President Trump’s speech to Congress, showing how petty fights and demands for retribution have become an increasingly prominent part of business on the House floor.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans unveil bill to avoid shutdown and they're daring Democrats to oppose it
item.description: House Republicans have unveiled a 99-page spending bill that would keep federal agencies funded through Sept. 30. It would provide a slight boost to defense programs while trimming nondefense programs below 2024 budget year levels.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tribes and Native American students sue over Bureau of Indian Education firings
item.description: Three tribal nations and five Native American students say in a lawsuit that the Trump administration has failed its legal obligations to tribes when it cut jobs at Bureau of Indian Education schools.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tribes and Native American students sue over Bureau of Indian Education firings
item.description: NORMAN, Okla. (AP) — Three tribal nations and five Native American students say in a lawsuit that the Trump administration has failed its legal obligations to tribes when it cut jobs at Bureau of Indian Education schools.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump is forcing a generational shift in GOP foreign policy. Here's how Republicans are responding
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans in Congress have long been intent on countering America's rivals and spreading U.S. influence abroad.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: Singing Into Irrelevancy
item.description: Gump logic: Forrest Gump’s mama was right – stupid is as stupid does. ICYMI: For those that don't know, ICYMI is how the cool kids say “In case you missed it.” Sibling shoutout: The smarter and more talented Vittert child – aka my sister, Liberty – is out with a must-read in The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Protesters denounce ICE arrests at St. Louis Park manufacturing facility
item.description: Dozens of people gathered outside a St. Louis Park aluminum finishing facility Wednesday night to protest recent federal immigration arrests at the company.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox and Regina Medina, MPR News

item.title: State Dept using AI to ban visas over perceived pro-Hamas posts
item.description: The initiative, started under Secretary of State Marco Rubio, will use AI to scan student visa holders’ social media accounts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: War heroes and military firsts are among 26,000 images flagged for removal in Pentagon's DEI purge
item.description: References to a World War II Medal of Honor recipient, the Enola Gay aircraft that dropped an atomic bomb on Japan and women and minorities are among the tens of thousands of photos and online posts marked for deletion as the Defense Department works to purge diversity, equity and inclusion content.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Targeted Tesla attacks continue amid outrage at Elon Musk
item.description: Some Tesla showrooms, dealerships, and charging stations have been set on fire or vandalized in other ways, ostensibly in protest of Musk's role.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, NewsNation

item.title: Tribal nations are concerned that Trump's cuts have the potential to violate trust responsibilities
item.description: Tribal leaders across the United States are scrambling to respond to a directive from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk to close more than a quarter of Bureau of Indian Affairs offices, which provide vital services to Indigenous communities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: War heroes and military firsts are among 26,000 images flagged for removal in Pentagon's DEI purge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — References to a World War II Medal of Honor recipient, the Enola Gay aircraft that dropped an atomic bomb on Japan and the first women to pass Marine infantry training are among the tens of thousands of photos and online posts marked for deletion as the Defense Department works to
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, LOLITA C. BALDOR and KEVIN VINEYS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Linda McMahon: Federal student aid could shift to another department
item.description: Newly confirmed Department of Education Secretary Linda McMahon told NewsNation that student loans, Pell Grants and other federal aid "might best be served in another department.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: A downbeat Minnesota budget forecast initiates lawmaker blame game
item.description: Minnesota lawmakers received an economic budget forecast that shows less wiggle room as they write a new budget and an even larger looming deficit in the future. Gov. Tim Walz and DFL leaders blamed President Donald Trump while Republican leaders said Democrats need to look in the mirror.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Students rally at Florida capitol opposing amendment petition reform
item.description: From the Panhandle to the Keys, hundreds of students from across Florida protested Thursday at the state capitol.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump is set to speak to crypto founders and investors at the White House on Friday
item.description: President Trump signed an order late Thursday creating a cryptocurrency strategic reserve using bitcoin seized through court proceedings, directing officials to find “budget-neutral” ways to grow it.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Former US solicitor general to submit written arguments in Eric Adams case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former U.S. solicitor general who was brought in to help a federal judge decide whether to accept a Justice Department request to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to submit written arguments Friday. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump stresses Cabinet secretaries lead agency cuts, but keeps Musk as threat
item.description: President Trump on Thursday stressed that his Cabinet secretaries take the lead on making cuts to their agencies and staff, insisting that officials are empowered to make decisions before Elon Musk can step in. “I want the Cabinet members, go first, keep all the people you want.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump passes Clinton, gives longest modern address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump gave the longest address to a joint session of Congress in modern history on Tuesday night, surpassing former President Bill Clinton’s previous mark.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump addresses joint session of Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A president's speech to Congress — even without the formal gloss of a State of the Union address — is typically a time for a call to national unity and predictable claims about the country being strong. But that wasn’t President Donald Trump’s plan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Proposal would use cameras to ticket double parking in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- A proposed pilot program aims to crack down on various curb violations, including drivers who double park in New York City. The program would use cameras to issue automatic tickets.
item.dc:creator: Kiran Dhillon, PIX 11

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration ramps up attacks as Zelenskyy thanks US
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, WFLA, NBC
